data.title: Apple's (AAPL) iPhone Sales Continue to Surge Worldwide
data.description:
Data shows that Apple's (AAPL) newest iPhone 17 continues to sell at a brisk pace.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Analysts at Wall Street brokerage Bernstein say that iPhone sales worldwide increased 26% year-over-year in February. iPhone 17 sales were 20% higher compared to sales of the previous iPhone 16 model between September and January, adds Bernstein.

The base model iPhone 17 accounted for 39% of the February growth, while the iPhone 17 Pro Max line comprised 21% of the increased sales. Also positive is the fact that the average selling price of the iPhone 17 in February was up 7% year-over-year. So far, prices for the iPhone 17 have risen each month since the device launched last September.

iPhone Sales in China

Bernstein said that the market in China provided the biggest boost to average selling price of the iPhone 17, with year-over-year growth of 20%. Apple reported record iPhone revenue of $85.3 billion in its most recent financial results.

The record sales were well ahead of Wall Street estimates that called for $78.3 billion in iPhone sales. The company saw iPhone sales of $69.1 billion in the same period a year earlier. Bernstein said its latest sales data bodes well for Apple's next financial results that are scheduled to be released on April 30.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

Apple stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 24 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $304.66 implies 22% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: AI Fears Are Rising, But Value Investors Aren't Letting Go of Microsoft and ServiceNow Stocks
data.description:
Investment firm BNP Paribas (BNPQY) says that value investors are still holding onto major enterprise software names like Microsoft (MSFT) and ServiceNow (NOW), even as concerns around artificial intelligence continue to grow. According to analyst Stefan Slowinski, the constant stream of negative news, along with weak sector performance, has made investors more cautious, with many now viewing the space as difficult to figure out. As a result, many investors are questioning whether they should stay invested in software stocks at all.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Indeed, Slowinski noted that most recent investor discussions are not about selling outright, but rather whether to continue holding positions. In those conversations, Microsoft and ServiceNow are the two names that come up most often. This suggests that while confidence has fallen, investors are still sticking with established companies for now. Nevertheless, some are worried that new AI tools could pressure pricing power and margins.

There is also growing concern that software companies could lose some of their value if they end up acting more like resellers of AI services rather than offering unique products. However, despite all this uncertainty, many still believe the biggest players are best positioned to win over time, especially given their past success in areas like cloud computing.

Which Software Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the two stocks mentioned above, analysts think that NOW stock has more room to run than MSFT. In fact, NOW's price target of $181.90 per share implies 81.4% upside versus MSFT's 57%.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Slides 42% in Six Months — Is It Time to Buy the Dip?
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) remains one of Wall Street's most closely watched AI-infrastructure stocks. Shares have fallen 42% over the past six months, erasing much of the post-IPO gains as investors reassess the company's rapid expansion, heavy capital spending, and uncertain path to profitability. Even so, CRWV's strong backlog, high‑profile AI deals, and strong growth outlook make the pullback look appealing to some investors. Importantly, CRWV stock is a Buy, according to the technical indicators, implying upside from current levels.

data.title: Why UBS Significantly Increased Its Price Target on Exxon Mobil (XOM) Stock
data.description:
Investment firm UBS (UBS) is bullish on Exxon Mobil (XOM). In fact, it kept a Buy rating and increased its price target from $145 to $171 per share as new risks emerge in the global helium market. The firm pointed to rising tensions in the Middle East, where Iran's drone and missile strikes on Qatar are disrupting supply. This matters because Qatar produces about 31% of the world's helium. Since helium is essential for things like MRI machines, rockets, and advanced chips, any disruption can have a big impact across multiple industries.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

At the same time, the situation is being made worse by shipping issues. The Strait of Hormuz, a key route for exports, is effectively closed to Western commercial shipping. Because helium must be transported in specialized containers by sea, there are currently no easy ways to move supplies out of the region. As a result, global markets are facing delays and tighter supply, which is increasing pressure on prices.

This is where Exxon could benefit. Five-star UBS analyst Manav Gupta noted that Exxon's LaBarge facility in Wyoming produces about 20% of the world's helium. With more than 30% of the global supply now disrupted, Exxon is in a strong position to help meet demand. In addition, the company may gain pricing power and offer a more reliable supply compared to competitors that depend on Qatar. As a result, UBS sees Exxon as well-positioned to benefit as the situation continues to develop.

Is XOM Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XOM stock based on 11 Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average XOM price target of $160.95 per share implies 1.8% downside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: LEVI Earnings: Levi Strauss Issues Strong Financial Results and Lifts Guidance
data.description:
Blue-jean maker Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) has reported better-than-expected financial results and raised its forward guidance, sending the stock up 6% in after-hours trading.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The San Francisco-based company announced earnings per share (EPS) of $0.42, which was ahead of the $0.37 expected on Wall Street. Revenue in the period totaled $1.74 billion, which topped the $1.65 billion consensus view of analysts who track the company's progress. Sales were up 14% from a year earlier.

The denim maker also lifted its forward guidance, saying it now expects full-year earnings of $1.42 to $1.48 per share, compared to Wall Street forecasts of $1.47 a share. The company anticipates its sales this year to rise between 5.5% and 6.5%, ahead of estimates that called for 5.6% revenue growth.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Oracle vs. Microsoft: Which Cloud Giant is the Better AI Play for 2026?
data.description:
As the race for artificial intelligence continues to heat up, Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL) have both emerged as major players, but in very different ways. Microsoft has long been a leader in cloud and AI integration, while Oracle has recently transformed itself into a fast-growing AI infrastructure provider. With both companies increasing their spending to meet rising AI demand, the key question becomes: which stock offers more upside in 2026? Interestingly, analysts think Oracle, but let's quickly compare the two before making any decisions.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Oracle: The High-Growth Challenger

We will begin with Oracle, which is no longer just a traditional database company. Instead, it has grown its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure business, with recent growth rates exceeding 60%. This surge has been driven by large contracts with companies like OpenAI and xAI. In addition, Oracle has built a massive backlog of future revenue, known as Remaining Performance Obligation, which now stands at about $553 billion. This gives investors strong visibility into future growth.

However, this expansion is expensive, as Oracle is spending nearly $50 billion on data centers. This has put pressure on its free cash flow. As a result, some are concerned about rising debt, while others believe Oracle is well-positioned to benefit from the growing demand for AI infrastructure. Nevertheless, Wall Street has an overall Strong Buy rating on Oracle stock with an average price target of $245.11 per share, which implies over 68% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Fed's Williams Signals Hold on Rates, Higher Inflation from Iran War
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank ‌of New York President ‌John Williams expects inflation to average around 2.75% this year as rising oil and gas prices contribute to higher prices. That supports the Fed maintaining rates between 3.50% and 3.75%, he told Bloomberg on Tuesday.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

The effects of the war "will ​directly go into ‌headline inflation because energy prices are an important component of that," said Williams.

Williams Sees Core Inflation Inching Higher, Cuts 2026 GDP Forecast

At the same time, he believes that core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, will only increase by 0.1% or 0.2% from current levels. "The story hasn't changed very much," he said.

However, Williams lowered his 2026 U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) forecast to between 2% and 2.5% from between 2.5% and 2.75%.

The Fed will meet on April 29 for its next interest rate decision, where it is widely expected to hold rates steady. Rates are expected to remain unchanged through year-end with 79.1% odds, according to the CME FedWatch tool.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Jump on Hopes for Diplomatic Progress with Iran
data.description:
U.S. stock futures surged on Tuesday evening, primarily due to hopes for a last-minute diplomatic resolution to the conflict with Iran, easing fears of severe oil supply disruptions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.95%, 0.82%, and 0.81%, respectively, at 6:15 p.m. EDT on April 7.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

In the regular trading session, U.S. stocks pared losses to finish mixed as President Donald Trump increased pressure on Iran ahead of his deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq each rose 0.1%, while the Dow slipped 0.2%.

Looking ahead, traders will turn their attention to the FOMC meeting minutes, set for release tomorrow. Markets use the minutes to gauge whether the Fed is leaning toward rate cuts, rate hikes, or staying on hold. On the earnings front, Delta Air Lines (DAL) will report its quarterly earnings on Wednesday.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Recover Losses as U.S.-Iran Weigh 2-Week Ceasefire ahead of Trump's Evening Deadline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) reversed losses and closed slightly higher after Pakistan proposed a two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump warned in a Truth Social post that Iran's "whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again." He added that a deal could be reached if Iran's new regime was willing to cooperate.
  • Following the post, Iran completely cut off direct communications with the U.S. At the same time, negotiations through mediators are still ongoing.
  • A U.S. official told Axios that optimism surrounding a deal has inched higher, with the attitude at the White House shifting from "can we get there?" to "can we get there by 8 o'clock tonight?"
  • Later in the day, Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said negotiations are "progressing steadily" and requested a two-week deadline extension, along with a two-week ceasefire and reopening of Hormuz as a gesture of goodwill.
  • White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt said Trump will soon provide a response to Sharif's proposal, while a senior Iranian official said Iran is reviewing the proposal.
  • U.S. consumers now expect year-ahead inflation of 3.4%, up from 3.0% in February.
  • Apple (AAPL) closed lower following reports that its foldable iPhone had run into engineering issues during early testing and could be delayed. However, AAPL recovered some of its midday losses after Bloomberg reported that the smartphone was still on track for a September release.
  • Home Depot (HD) closed lower after Jim Cramer said he was "very disappointed" in the home improvement company, citing high mortgage rates and its involvement with ICE.
  • Anthropic announced a multibillion dollar AI chip deal with Google (GOOGL) and Broadcom (AVGO).

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Communication services was the best-performing sector on Tuesday with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) posting a 10% return following news that three Middle Eastern sovereign-wealth funds would back its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). The sector also benefited from a market-wide recovery as investors shifted capital into risk-on assets.

Several communication services stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY)
  • Google (GOOGL)
  • Charter Communications (CHTR)
  • T-Mobile (TMUS)
  • Take-Two Interactive (TTWO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of defensive names amid growing optimism for a U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Campbell's (CPB) led to the downside on fears that it could be removed from the S&P 500 (SPX).

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Campbell's (CPB)
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR)
  • Kimberly-Clark (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
  • Kenvue (KVUE)
data.author: Eddie Pan
